zh.Croft, Herefordshire, England: 英國英格蘭賀福爾卡芙特
zh.Ramsbury, Wiltshire, England: 英國維爾爾拉姆斯波利
zh.Littlecote, Ramsbury, Wiltshire, England: 英國英格蘭維爾爾拉姆斯波利
zh.lifeSummary: 當Jane Croft於1470年出生在英國維爾爾拉姆斯波利時，她的父親Richard de Croft43歲，母親Jane Hebert42歲。她於1487年與Sir Edward Darell在英國英格蘭維爾爾拉姆斯波利結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和3個女兒的父母。她去世於1536年10月8日在她的家鄉享年66歲，埋葬在英國維爾爾拉姆斯波利。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Jane Croft于1470年出生在英国維爾爾拉姆斯波利时，她的父亲Richard de Croft43岁，母亲Jane Hebert42岁。她于1487年与Sir Edward Darell在英国英格兰維爾爾拉姆斯波利结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和3个女儿的父母。她去世于1536年10月8日在她的家乡享年66岁，埋葬在英国維爾爾拉姆斯波利。

nameMeanings.name: Croft
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from any of several places, for example in Lincolnshire, Lancashire, Herefordshire, and North Yorkshire, all named with Old English croft ‘small arable enclosure (normally adjoining a house)’, or a topographic name for someone who lived by a croft, from Middle English croft.
English: possibly in some cases a variant of   Craft   2.
Americanized form of German, Danish, Swedish, or Jewish   Kraft  . Compare   Kroft   and   Craft  .

variants.name: Craft
meanings: English: nickname from Middle English craft ‘skillful, clever, learned’.
English: habitational name from Croft in Leicestershire. See also   Croft  .
Americanized form of German, Danish, Swedish, or Jewish   Kraft  . See also   Croft  .

variants.name: Crate
meanings: English (Hampshire and Surrey): from Middle English crate ‘hurdle, basket’, or occasionally perhaps from crete ‘cradle’, probably applied as a metonymic occupational name for a maker of such articles. Alternatively, sometimes perhaps a variant of Croft 1.

variants.name: Crofts
meanings: English: variant of Croft , with post-medieval excrescent -s.

variants.name: Groft
meanings: English: perhaps an altered form of   Croft  .

nameMeanings.name: Jane
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

variants.name: Jayne
meanings: Elaborated spelling of   Jane  , a form popularized no doubt by the American actress  Jayne Mansfield  (  Vera Jayne Palmer ,  1933–67 ) and British ice-dancer  Jayne Torvill  (  b. 1957 ).

variants.name: Jain
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

en.lifeSummary: When Jane Croft was born in 1470, in Ramsbury, Wiltshire, England, her father, Richard de Croft, was 43 and her mother, Jane Hebert, was 42. She married Sir Edward Darell in 1487, in Ramsbury, Wiltshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 3 daughters. She died on 8 October 1536, in her hometown, at the age of 66, and was buried in Ramsbury, Wiltshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Jane Croft est née en 1470, à Ramsbury, Wiltshire, Angleterre, son père, Richard de Croft, avait 43 ans et sa mère, Jane Hebert, avait 42 ans. Elle a épousé Sir Edward Darell en 1487, à Ramsbury, Wiltshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 3 filles. Elle est décédée le 8 octobre 1536, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 66 ans, et a été enterrée à Ramsbury, Wiltshire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Jane Croft 1470 in Ramsbury, Wiltshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Richard de Croft 43 und ihre Mutter Jane Hebert 42. Sie hat 1487 in Ramsbury, Wiltshire, England, United Kingdom Sir Edward Darell geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 3 Töchter. Sie starb am 8. Oktober 1536 im Alter von 66 Jahren in ihrem Geburtsort und wurde in Ramsbury, Wiltshire, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Jane Croft è nata il nel 1470 nel Ramsbury, Wiltshire, England, sua padre, Richard de Croft, aveva 43 anni e sua madre, Jane Hebert, aveva 42 anni. Lei sposato Sir Edward Darell nel 1487, nel Ramsbury, Wiltshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 3 figlie. Morì il 8 ottobre 1536, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 66 anni, e fu sepolta a Ramsbury, Wiltshire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Jane Croftが1470年にイギリスウィルトシュアイアラムスブリーで生まれた時，父Richard de Croftは43歳，母Jane Hebertは42歳であった。1487年にイギリスイギリスウィルトシュアイアラムスブリーでSir Edward Darellと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼女は1536年10月8日に故郷で66歳で死去し，イギリスウィルトシュアイアラムスブリーにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Jane Croft(이)가 1470년 영국 윌츠히르 램스버이에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Richard de Croft(은)는 43세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Jane Hebert(은)는 42세였다. 그녀는 1487년 영국 잉글랜드 윌츠히르 램스버이에서 Sir Edward Darell(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1536년 10월 8일 그녀의 고향에서 66세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 윌츠히르 램스버이에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Jane Croft nasceu em 1470, em Ramsbury, Wiltshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Richard de Croft, tinha 43 anos e sua mãe, Jane Hebert, tinha 42 anos. Ela casou-se com Sir Edward Darell em 1487, em Ramsbury, Wiltshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 3 filhas. Ela faleceu em 8 de outubro de 1536, em sua cidade natal, com 66 anos, e foi sepultada em Ramsbury, Wiltshire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Jane Croft родилась в 1470 г. в местности рамсбури, уилцхир, Англия, её отцу по имени Richard de Croft было 43 года, а матери, которую звали Jane Hebert, 42. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir Edward Darell в 1487 г. в местности рамсбури, уилцхир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 3 дочери. Она умерла 8 октября 1536 г. в её родном городе в возрасте 66 лет и была похоронена в местности рамсбури, уилцхир, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Jane Croft nació en 1470, en Ramsbury, Wiltshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Richard de Croft, tenía 43 años y su mamá, Jane Hebert, tenía 42 años. Se casó con Sir Edward Darell en 1487, en Ramsbury, Wiltshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 3 hijas. Murió el 8 de octubre de 1536, en su pueblo, a la edad de 66 años, y fue sepultada en Ramsbury, Wiltshire, Inglaterra.
